ranked_matches:
Here’s the ranked list of the **TOP 10 jobs** from **BEST to WORST match** for your candidate, based on skill overlap, role alignment, and missing requirements:

---

### **1. Machine Learning Engineer - AI (Remote) at Hire Feed**
   **Match Score: 95/100**
   **Why:** Perfect fit for a **Machine Learning Engineer**—covers all core skills (Python, Scikit-Learn, Pandas, TF-IDF, Cosine Similarity, Feature Engineering) and adds **Streamlit** for deployment.
   **Missing:** No explicit mention of **PyTorch/TensorFlow** (though Scikit-Learn is sufficient for many ML tasks).
   **Apply:** [LinkedIn](https://pk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/machine-learning-engineer-ai-remote-at-hire-feed-4434725484)

---

### **2. Machine Learning (AI) Engineer (Hybrid) at Hire Feed**
   **Match Score: 94/100**
   **Why:** Nearly identical to #1, with **hybrid flexibility**—ideal for a mid-level ML Engineer with **Streamlit** and visualization skills.
   **Missing:** No mention of **cloud deployment** (e.g., AWS/GCP) or **MLOps pipelines** (though Streamlit helps).
   **Apply:** [LinkedIn](https://pk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/machine-learning-ai-engineer-hybrid-at-hire-feed-4435514024)

---

### **3. Data Scientist - AI/ML (Remote) at Hire Feed**
   **Match Score: 90/100**
   **Why:** Strong **Data Scientist** alignment—covers **Python, Scikit-Learn, TF-IDF, Feature Engineering**, and **visualization (Matplotlib/Seaborn)**.
   **Missing:** Lacks **deep NLP** (e.g., BERT) or **production ML** (though remote flexibility is a plus).
   **Apply:** [LinkedIn](https://pk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-scientist-ai-ml-remote-at-hire-feed-4434924820)

---

### **4. Artificial Intelligence Engineer (LearnWith.AI) at Crossover**
   **Match Score: 88/100**
   **Why:** High-paying **AI Engineer** role with **Python, Scikit-Learn, and TF-IDF**—great for **NLP/text-based ML** (though remote).
   **Missing:** **PyTorch/TensorFlow** not explicitly listed (but Scikit-Learn is sufficient for many tasks).
   **Apply:** [LinkedIn](https://pk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/artificial-intelligence-engineer-learnwith-ai-remote-%24200-000-year-usd-at-crossover-4431434060)

---

### **5. Artificial Intelligence Specialist at Recruitzz (Lahore)**
   **Match Score: 85/100**
   **Why:** **Local Lahore role** with **Python, Scikit-Learn, and TF-IDF**—good for **on-site collaboration** and **feature engineering**.
   **Missing:** No mention of **ML deployment** (Streamlit could bridge this).
   **Apply:** [LinkedIn](https://pk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/artificial-intelligence-specialist-at-recruitzz-4431505029)

---

### **6. Machine Learning Researcher (Remote) at Hire Feed**
   **Match Score: 82/100**
   **Why:** Research-focused but still aligns with **Python, Scikit-Learn, and TF-IDF**—good for **experimental ML**.
   **Missing:** **Academic/research-specific tools** (e.g., Jupyter notebooks, LaTeX) not mentioned.
   **Apply:** [LinkedIn](https://pk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/machine-learning-researcher-remote-at-hire-feed-4434488174)

---

### **7. ML Engineer at AI Startup (San Francisco)**
   **Match Score: 80/100**
   **Why:** **High-potential startup role** with **Python, TF-IDF, and Streamlit**—but **location is SF (not Pakistan)**.
   **Missing:** **PyTorch** (required) and **NLP production deployment** (candidate lacks this).
   **Apply:** [Example](https://example.com/2) *(Note: URL placeholder—verify)*

---

### **8. Junior Research Analyst at Louis Dreyfus Company**
   **Match Score: 70/100**
   **Why:** **Pandas, Matplotlib, and TF-IDF** fit **research/analysis**, but **less ML-focused** than top roles.
   **Missing:** **No Scikit-Learn, Feature Engineering, or Streamlit** (core ML skills).
   **Apply:** [LinkedIn](https://pk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/junior-research-analyst-at-louis-dreyfus-company-4431505931)

---

### **9. Entry-Level Software Developer at Recruit AI**
   **Match Score: 50/100**
   **Why:** **Python** is listed, but **no ML/DS tools** (e.g., TensorFlow, Flask) are required—**not a good fit**.
   **Missing:** **All ML-specific skills** (Scikit-Learn, TF-IDF, etc.) are irrelevant here.
   **Apply:** [LinkedIn](https://pk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/entry-level-software-developer-tj-1857341-at-recruit-ai-4435108400)

---

### **10. Machine Learning (AI) Engineer (Hybrid) at Hire Feed (Duplicate)**
   **Match Score: 75/100** *(Same as #2, but listed twice—likely a duplicate)*
   **Why:** Redundant with #2—**same skills, same role**.
   **Missing:** No unique advantages over #2.
   **Apply:** [LinkedIn](https://pk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/machine-learning-ai-engineer-hybrid-at-hire-feed-4434724165)

---

### **Key Takeaways:**
1. **Top 3 roles (#1–#3)** are **perfect fits** for a **Machine Learning Engineer/Data Scientist** with **Python, Scikit-Learn, and Streamlit**.
2. **Local vs. Remote:** If **Lahore-based** roles are preferred, **#5 (Recruitzz)** is a strong local option.
3. **Avoid #9 & #10:** **#9 is a software dev role (not ML)**, and **#10 is a duplicate**.
4. **Highest Salary:** **#4 (Crossover, $200K USD)** is a **luxury remote role** but may require **PyTorch experience** (not listed in candidate’s skills).

**Recommendation:** Apply to **#1, #2, #3, and #5** first, then negotiate for **remote flexibility** if possible.

jobs.title: Machine Learning Engineer - AI (Remote)
jobs.description: Job Title: Machine Learning Engineer - AI (Remote) | Company: Hire Feed | Location: Pakistan
jobs.match_reason: Strong alignment with **Machine Learning Engineer** role: Python, Scikit-Learn, Pandas, NumPy, TF-IDF Vectorization, Cosine Similarity, and Feature Engineering are core ML skills. Streamlit and visualization tools (Matplotlib/Seaborn) support deployment and model interpretation, while remote work flexibility matches the candidate’s flexibility.

Streamlit adds a bonus for potential ML app development.
